---
title: "nitrix.linalg.matrix_function"
generated: true
signature-hash: d9a094c56391f1b5
description: "Matrix functions of a *general* (non-symmetric) matrix."
listing:
  id: nitrix-linalg-matrix_function-symbols
  contents: "nitrix-linalg-matrix_function/*.qmd"
  type: table
  fields: [title, description]
  sort: "title"
  filter-ui: false
  page-size: 500
---

::: {.callout-important title="Generated reference stub — fill me"}
Auto-generated scaffold for `nitrix.linalg.matrix_function`. Fill the prose *outside* the
generated markers; do **not** ship as-is (SPEC §6.5). `check_reference_staleness.py` flags signature drift.
:::

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:__module__ -->
Matrix functions of a *general* (non-symmetric) matrix.

The symmetric family ([`symexp`](nitrix-linalg-spd/symexp.qmd) /
[`symlog`](nitrix-linalg-spd/symlog.qmd) / [`symsqrt`](nitrix-linalg-spd/symsqrt.qmd) /
[`sympower`](nitrix-linalg-spd/sympower.qmd)) applies a scalar function to the
eigenvalues of a symmetric matrix.  [`matrix_exp`](nitrix-linalg-matrix_function/matrix_exp.qmd) is the general-matrix
counterpart: the true matrix exponential $e^{A}$ for an arbitrary square
$A$, needed to exponentiate a non-symmetric Lie-algebra element --
specifically the affine generator $[[A, b], [0, 0]]$ whose exponential is
the affine transform [`affine_exp`](nitrix-geometry-transform/affine_exp.qmd) applies (a
rigid generator's rotation block is skew-symmetric, also non-symmetric).
[`matrix_log`](nitrix-linalg-matrix_function/matrix_log.qmd) is the inverse, warranted by the affine Fréchet/Karcher mean
[`transform_mean`](nitrix-geometry-algebra/transform_mean.qmd).

[`matrix_exp`](nitrix-linalg-matrix_function/matrix_exp.qmd) uses **scaling-and-squaring with a truncated Taylor
series**.  Unlike `jax.scipy.linalg.expm` (Padé, which needs a linear solve
and so wedges / falls back to CPU on the affected cuSolver stacks), this path is
**pure matmul** -- GPU-native, `jit` / `grad`-clean, and never mixes
devices inside a traced optimiser.  $A$ is scaled by $2^{-s}$, the
Taylor series of $e^{A/2^{s}}$ is summed, and the result is squared
$s$ times.  With the defaults ($s = 8$, order 12) the scaled norm is
tiny for any $\lVert A \rVert$ that arises from a registration generator,
so the result matches a reference `expm` to machine precision; raise them for
very large $\lVert A \rVert$.

[`matrix_log`](nitrix-linalg-matrix_function/matrix_log.qmd) is the inverse: **inverse scaling-and-squaring** -- take
$s$ matrix square roots (Denman--Beavers) to drive $A$ toward
$I$, sum the Taylor series of $\log(I + X)$ on the small remainder,
and multiply by $2^{s}$.  Registration affines carry large translations
($\lVert A - I \rVert \gg 1$), which rules out a pure-matmul (binomial)
square root, so the Denman--Beavers step routes its inverse through
`safe_inv` (GPU-native on a healthy stack;
CPU-fallback on the wedged-cuSolver dev box).  Unlike [`matrix_exp`](nitrix-linalg-matrix_function/matrix_exp.qmd) this is
**not** for an optimiser hot loop -- it backs the offline Karcher mean -- so the
fallback is acceptable.  An out-of-domain input (an eigenvalue on the negative
real axis) returns **NaN**, not finite garbage, caught by a cuSolver-free
round-trip-through-[`matrix_exp`](nitrix-linalg-matrix_function/matrix_exp.qmd) guard (see [`matrix_log`](nitrix-linalg-matrix_function/matrix_log.qmd)).
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen dims:__page__ -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `...` | anonymous leading (batch) axes |
| `n` | query points, each an `ndim`-vector coordinate (variadic) |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## Symbols

::: {#nitrix-linalg-matrix_function-symbols}
:::
